documentation: Python runtime library for use with the `Flatbuffers <https://google.github.io/flatbuffers/>`_ serialization format.

functions.name: flatbuffers.encode.Get
documentation: Get decodes a value at buf[head] using `packer_type`.

functions.name: flatbuffers.encode.GetVectorAsNumpy
documentation:
GetVecAsNumpy decodes values starting at buf[head] as
`numpy_type`, where `numpy_type` is a numpy dtype.

functions.name: flatbuffers.encode.Write
documentation: Write encodes `n` at buf[head] using `packer_type`.

classes.name: flatbuffers.table.Table

methods.name: flatbuffers.table.Table.Offset
documentation:
Offset provides access into the Table's vtable.

Deprecated fields are ignored by checking the vtable's length.

methods.name: flatbuffers.table.Table.Indirect
documentation: Indirect retrieves the relative offset stored at `offset`.

methods.name: flatbuffers.table.Table.String
documentation: String gets a string from data stored inside the flatbuffer.

methods.name: flatbuffers.table.Table.VectorLen
documentation:
VectorLen retrieves the length of the vector whose offset is stored
at "off" in this object.

methods.name: flatbuffers.table.Table.Vector
documentation:
Vector retrieves the start of data of the vector whose offset is
stored at "off" in this object.

methods.name: flatbuffers.table.Table.Union
documentation:
Union initializes any Table-derived type to point to the union at
the given offset.

methods.name: flatbuffers.table.Table.Get
documentation:
Get retrieves a value of the type specified by `flags`  at the
given offset.

methods.name: flatbuffers.table.Table.GetVectorAsNumpy
documentation:
GetVectorAsNumpy returns the vector that starts at `Vector(off)`
as a numpy array with the type specified by `flags`. The array is
a `view` into Bytes, so modifying the returned array will
modify Bytes in place.

methods.name: flatbuffers.table.Table.GetArrayAsNumpy
documentation:
GetArrayAsNumpy returns the array with fixed width that starts at `Vector(offset)`
with length `length` as a numpy array with the type specified by `flags`. The
array is a `view` into Bytes so modifying the returned will modify Bytes in place.

methods.name: flatbuffers.table.Table.GetVOffsetTSlot
documentation:
GetVOffsetTSlot retrieves the VOffsetT that the given vtable location
points to. If the vtable value is zero, the default value `d`
will be returned.

Table wraps a byte slice and provides read access to its data.

The variable `Pos` indicates the root of the FlatBuffers object therein.

functions.name: flatbuffers.builder.vtableEqual
documentation: vtableEqual compares an unwritten vtable to a written vtable.
# @cond FLATBUFFERS_INTERNAL

classes.name: flatbuffers.builder.OffsetArithmeticError
documentation:
Error caused by an Offset arithmetic error. Probably caused by bad
writing of fields. This is considered an unreachable situation in
normal circumstances.
# @file
# @addtogroup flatbuffers_python_api
# @cond FLATBUFFERS_INTERNAL

classes.name: flatbuffers.builder.IsNotNestedError
documentation:
Error caused by using a Builder to write Object data when not inside
an Object.

classes.name: flatbuffers.builder.IsNestedError
documentation:
Error caused by using a Builder to begin an Object when an Object is
already being built.

classes.name: flatbuffers.builder.StructIsNotInlineError
documentation:
Error caused by using a Builder to write a Struct at a location that
is not the current Offset.

classes.name: flatbuffers.builder.BuilderSizeError
documentation:
Error caused by causing a Builder to exceed the hardcoded limit of 2
gigabytes.

classes.name: flatbuffers.builder.BuilderNotFinishedError
documentation: Error caused by not calling `Finish` before calling `Output`.

classes.name: flatbuffers.builder.EndVectorLengthMismatched
documentation:
The number of elements passed to EndVector does not match the number 
specified in StartVector.

classes.name: flatbuffers.builder.Builder

methods.name: flatbuffers.builder.Builder.__init__
documentation:
Initializes a Builder of size `initial_size`.

The internal buffer is grown as needed.
# @endcond

methods.name: flatbuffers.builder.Builder.Output
documentation:
Return the portion of the buffer that has been used for writing data.

This is the typical way to access the FlatBuffer data inside the
builder. If you try to access `Builder.Bytes` directly, you would need
to manually index it with `Head()`, since the buffer is constructed
backwards.

It raises BuilderNotFinishedError if the buffer has not been finished
with `Finish`.

methods.name: flatbuffers.builder.Builder.StartObject
documentation: StartObject initializes bookkeeping for writing a new object.
# @cond FLATBUFFERS_INTERNAL

methods.name: flatbuffers.builder.Builder.WriteVtable
documentation:
WriteVtable serializes the vtable for the current object, if needed.

Before writing out the vtable, this checks pre-existing vtables for
equality to this one. If an equal vtable is found, point the object to
the existing vtable and return.

Because vtable values are sensitive to alignment of object data, not
all logically-equal vtables will be deduplicated.

A vtable has the following format:
  <VOffsetT: size of the vtable in bytes, including this value>
  <VOffsetT: size of the object in bytes, including the vtable offset>
  <VOffsetT: offset for a field> * N, where N is the number of fields
             in the schema for this type. Includes deprecated fields.
Thus, a vtable is made of 2 + N elements, each VOffsetT bytes wide.

An object has the following format:
  <SOffsetT: offset to this object's vtable (may be negative)>
  <byte: data>+

methods.name: flatbuffers.builder.Builder.EndObject
documentation: EndObject writes data necessary to finish object construction.

methods.name: flatbuffers.builder.Builder.growByteBuffer
documentation:
Doubles the size of the byteslice, and copies the old data towards
the end of the new buffer (since we build the buffer backwards).

methods.name: flatbuffers.builder.Builder.Head
documentation:
Get the start of useful data in the underlying byte buffer.

Note: unlike other functions, this value is interpreted as from the
left.
# @endcond

methods.name: flatbuffers.builder.Builder.Offset
documentation: Offset relative to the end of the buffer.
# @cond FLATBUFFERS_INTERNAL

methods.name: flatbuffers.builder.Builder.Pad
documentation: Pad places zeros at the current offset.

methods.name: flatbuffers.builder.Builder.Prep
documentation:
Prep prepares to write an element of `size` after `additional_bytes`
have been written, e.g. if you write a string, you need to align
such the int length field is aligned to SizeInt32, and the string
data follows it directly.
If all you need to do is align, `additionalBytes` will be 0.

methods.name: flatbuffers.builder.Builder.PrependSOffsetTRelative
documentation:
PrependSOffsetTRelative prepends an SOffsetT, relative to where it
will be written.

methods.name: flatbuffers.builder.Builder.PrependUOffsetTRelative
documentation:
Prepends an unsigned offset into vector data, relative to where it
will be written.
# @endcond

methods.name: flatbuffers.builder.Builder.StartVector
documentation:
StartVector initializes bookkeeping for writing a new vector.

A vector has the following format:
  - <UOffsetT: number of elements in this vector>
  - <T: data>+, where T is the type of elements of this vector.
# @cond FLATBUFFERS_INTERNAL

methods.name: flatbuffers.builder.Builder.EndVector
documentation: EndVector writes data necessary to finish vector construction.
# @endcond

methods.name: flatbuffers.builder.Builder.CreateSharedString
documentation:
CreateSharedString checks if the string is already written to the buffer
before calling CreateString.

methods.name: flatbuffers.builder.Builder.CreateString
documentation: CreateString writes a null-terminated byte string as a vector.

methods.name: flatbuffers.builder.Builder.CreateByteVector
documentation: CreateString writes a byte vector.

methods.name: flatbuffers.builder.Builder.CreateNumpyVector
documentation: CreateNumpyVector writes a numpy array into the buffer.

methods.name: flatbuffers.builder.Builder.assertNested
documentation: Check that we are in the process of building an object.
# @cond FLATBUFFERS_INTERNAL

methods.name: flatbuffers.builder.Builder.assertNotNested
documentation:
Check that no other objects are being built while making this
object. If not, raise an exception.

methods.name: flatbuffers.builder.Builder.assertStructIsInline
documentation:
Structs are always stored inline, so need to be created right
where they are used. You'll get this error if you created it
elsewhere.

methods.name: flatbuffers.builder.Builder.Slot
documentation:
Slot sets the vtable key `voffset` to the current location in the
buffer.

methods.name: flatbuffers.builder.Builder.__Finish
documentation: Finish finalizes a buffer, pointing to the given `rootTable`.
# @endcond

methods.name: flatbuffers.builder.Builder.Finish
documentation: Finish finalizes a buffer, pointing to the given `rootTable`.

methods.name: flatbuffers.builder.Builder.FinishSizePrefixed
documentation:
Finish finalizes a buffer, pointing to the given `rootTable`,
with the size prefixed.

methods.name: flatbuffers.builder.Builder.Prepend
documentation: # @cond FLATBUFFERS_INTERNAL

methods.name: flatbuffers.builder.Builder.PrependUOffsetTRelativeSlot
documentation:
PrependUOffsetTRelativeSlot prepends an UOffsetT onto the object at
vtable slot `o`. If value `x` equals default `d`, then the slot will
be set to zero and no other data will be written.

methods.name: flatbuffers.builder.Builder.PrependStructSlot
documentation:
PrependStructSlot prepends a struct onto the object at vtable slot `o`.
Structs are stored inline, so nothing additional is being added.
In generated code, `d` is always 0.

methods.name: flatbuffers.builder.Builder.PrependBool
documentation:
Prepend a `bool` to the Builder buffer.

Note: aligns and checks for space.
# @endcond

methods.name: flatbuffers.builder.Builder.PrependByte
documentation:
Prepend a `byte` to the Builder buffer.

Note: aligns and checks for space.

methods.name: flatbuffers.builder.Builder.PrependUint8
documentation:
Prepend an `uint8` to the Builder buffer.

Note: aligns and checks for space.

methods.name: flatbuffers.builder.Builder.PrependUint16
documentation:
Prepend an `uint16` to the Builder buffer.

Note: aligns and checks for space.

methods.name: flatbuffers.builder.Builder.PrependUint32
documentation:
Prepend an `uint32` to the Builder buffer.

Note: aligns and checks for space.

methods.name: flatbuffers.builder.Builder.PrependUint64
documentation:
Prepend an `uint64` to the Builder buffer.

Note: aligns and checks for space.

methods.name: flatbuffers.builder.Builder.PrependInt8
documentation:
Prepend an `int8` to the Builder buffer.

Note: aligns and checks for space.

methods.name: flatbuffers.builder.Builder.PrependInt16
documentation:
Prepend an `int16` to the Builder buffer.

Note: aligns and checks for space.

methods.name: flatbuffers.builder.Builder.PrependInt32
documentation:
Prepend an `int32` to the Builder buffer.

Note: aligns and checks for space.

methods.name: flatbuffers.builder.Builder.PrependInt64
documentation:
Prepend an `int64` to the Builder buffer.

Note: aligns and checks for space.

methods.name: flatbuffers.builder.Builder.PrependFloat32
documentation:
Prepend a `float32` to the Builder buffer.

Note: aligns and checks for space.

methods.name: flatbuffers.builder.Builder.PrependFloat64
documentation:
Prepend a `float64` to the Builder buffer.

Note: aligns and checks for space.

methods.name: flatbuffers.builder.Builder.ForceDefaults
documentation:
In order to save space, fields that are set to their default value
don't get serialized into the buffer. Forcing defaults provides a
way to manually disable this optimization. When set to `True`, will
always serialize default values.

methods.name: flatbuffers.builder.Builder.PrependVOffsetT
documentation: # @cond FLATBUFFERS_INTERNAL

methods.name: flatbuffers.builder.Builder.Place
documentation:
Place prepends a value specified by `flags` to the Builder,
without checking for available space.

methods.name: flatbuffers.builder.Builder.PlaceVOffsetT
documentation:
PlaceVOffsetT prepends a VOffsetT to the Builder, without checking
for space.

methods.name: flatbuffers.builder.Builder.PlaceSOffsetT
documentation:
PlaceSOffsetT prepends a SOffsetT to the Builder, without checking
for space.

methods.name: flatbuffers.builder.Builder.PlaceUOffsetT
documentation:
PlaceUOffsetT prepends a UOffsetT to the Builder, without checking
for space.

class_variables.name: flatbuffers.builder.Builder.__slots__
documentation:
Maximum buffer size constant, in bytes.

Builder will never allow it's buffer grow over this size.
Currently equals 2Gb.

A Builder is used to construct one or more FlatBuffers.

Typically, Builder objects will be used from code generated by the `flatc`
compiler.

A Builder constructs byte buffers in a last-first manner for simplicity and
performance during reading.

Internally, a Builder is a state machine for creating FlatBuffer objects.

It holds the following internal state:
    - Bytes: an array of bytes.
    - current_vtable: a list of integers.
    - vtables: a hash of vtable entries.

Attributes:
  Bytes: The internal `bytearray` for the Builder.
  finished: A boolean determining if the Builder has been finalized.
# @endcond

modules.name: flatbuffers.flexbuffers
documentation:
Implementation of FlexBuffers binary format.

For more info check https://google.github.io/flatbuffers/flexbuffers.html and
corresponding C++ implementation at
https://github.com/google/flatbuffers/blob/master/include/flatbuffers/flexbuffers.h
Lint as: python3
Copyright 2020 Google Inc. All rights reserved.
Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
You may obtain a copy of the License at
http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
limitations under the License.

functions.name: flatbuffers.flexbuffers._PaddingBytes
documentation: Computes how many bytes you'd have to pad to be able to write an
"scalar_size" scalar if the buffer had grown to "buf_size",
"scalar_size" is a power of two.

functions.name: flatbuffers.flexbuffers._LowerBound
documentation: Implementation of C++ std::lower_bound() algorithm.
https://en.cppreference.com/w/cpp/algorithm/lower_bound

functions.name: flatbuffers.flexbuffers._BinarySearch
documentation: Implementation of C++ std::binary_search() algorithm.
https://en.cppreference.com/w/cpp/algorithm/binary_search

functions.name: flatbuffers.flexbuffers.GetRoot
documentation: Returns root `Ref` object for the given buffer.

functions.name: flatbuffers.flexbuffers.Dumps
documentation: Returns bytearray with the encoded python object.

functions.name: flatbuffers.flexbuffers.Loads
documentation: Returns python object decoded from the buffer.

variables.name: flatbuffers.flexbuffers.I
documentation: Integer formats

variables.name: flatbuffers.flexbuffers.U
documentation: Unsigned integer formats

variables.name: flatbuffers.flexbuffers.F
documentation: Floating point formats

classes.name: flatbuffers.flexbuffers.BitWidth

methods.name: flatbuffers.flexbuffers.BitWidth.U
documentation: Returns the minimum `BitWidth` to encode unsigned integer value.

methods.name: flatbuffers.flexbuffers.BitWidth.I
documentation: Returns the minimum `BitWidth` to encode signed integer value.

methods.name: flatbuffers.flexbuffers.BitWidth.F
documentation: Returns the `BitWidth` to encode floating point value.

class_variables.name: flatbuffers.flexbuffers.BitWidth.W8
documentation: 2^0 = 1 byte

class_variables.name: flatbuffers.flexbuffers.BitWidth.W16
documentation: 2^1 = 2 bytes

class_variables.name: flatbuffers.flexbuffers.BitWidth.W32
documentation: 2^2 = 4 bytes

class_variables.name: flatbuffers.flexbuffers.BitWidth.W64
documentation: 2^3 = 8 bytes

Supported bit widths of value types.

These are used in the lower 2 bits of a type field to determine the size of
the elements (and or size field) of the item pointed to (e.g. vector).

classes.name: flatbuffers.flexbuffers.Type

methods.name: flatbuffers.flexbuffers.Type.ToTypedVector
documentation:
Converts element type to corresponding vector type.

Args:
  element_type: vector element type
  fixed_len: number of elements: 0 for typed vector; 2, 3, or 4 for fixed
    typed vector.

Returns:
  Typed vector type or fixed typed vector type.

class_variables.name: flatbuffers.flexbuffers.Type.VECTOR
documentation: Untyped.

class_variables.name: flatbuffers.flexbuffers.Type.VECTOR_INT
documentation: Typed any size (stores no type table).

class_variables.name: flatbuffers.flexbuffers.Type.VECTOR_INT2
documentation: Typed tuple (no type table, no size field).

class_variables.name: flatbuffers.flexbuffers.Type.VECTOR_INT3
documentation: Typed triple (no type table, no size field).

class_variables.name: flatbuffers.flexbuffers.Type.VECTOR_INT4
documentation: Typed quad (no type table, no size field).

class_variables.name: flatbuffers.flexbuffers.Type.VECTOR_BOOL
documentation: To do the same type of conversion of type to vector type

Supported types of encoded data.

These are used as the upper 6 bits of a type field to indicate the actual
type.

classes.name: flatbuffers.flexbuffers.Buf

methods.name: flatbuffers.flexbuffers.Buf.Find
documentation: Returns the lowest index where the sub subsequence is found.

methods.name: flatbuffers.flexbuffers.Buf.Slice
documentation: Returns new `Buf` which starts from the given offset.

methods.name: flatbuffers.flexbuffers.Buf.Indirect
documentation: Return new `Buf` based on the encoded offset (indirect encoding).
Class to access underlying buffer object starting from the given offset.

classes.name: flatbuffers.flexbuffers.Object
documentation: Base class for all non-trivial data accessors.

classes.name: flatbuffers.flexbuffers.Sized
documentation: Base class for all data accessors which need to read encoded size.

classes.name: flatbuffers.flexbuffers.Blob
documentation: Data accessor for the encoded blob bytes.

classes.name: flatbuffers.flexbuffers.String

methods.name: flatbuffers.flexbuffers.String.Mutate
documentation:
Mutates underlying string bytes in place.

Args:
  value: New string to replace the existing one. New string must have less
    or equal UTF-8-encoded bytes than the existing one to successfully
    mutate underlying byte buffer.

Returns:
  Whether the value was mutated or not.
Data accessor for the encoded string bytes.

classes.name: flatbuffers.flexbuffers.Key
documentation: Data accessor for the encoded key bytes.

classes.name: flatbuffers.flexbuffers.Vector

methods.name: flatbuffers.flexbuffers.Vector.Value
documentation: Returns the underlying encoded data as a list object.
Data accessor for the encoded vector bytes.

classes.name: flatbuffers.flexbuffers.TypedVector

methods.name: flatbuffers.flexbuffers.TypedVector.Value
documentation: Returns underlying data as list object.
Data accessor for the encoded typed vector or fixed typed vector bytes.

classes.name: flatbuffers.flexbuffers.Map
documentation: Data accessor for the encoded map bytes.

classes.name: flatbuffers.flexbuffers.Ref

methods.name: flatbuffers.flexbuffers.Ref.MutateBool
documentation:
Mutates underlying boolean value bytes in place.

Args:
  value: New boolean value.

Returns:
  Whether the value was mutated or not.

methods.name: flatbuffers.flexbuffers.Ref.AsInt
documentation: Returns current reference as integer value.

methods.name: flatbuffers.flexbuffers.Ref.MutateInt
documentation:
Mutates underlying integer value bytes in place.

Args:
  value: New integer value. It must fit to the byte size of the existing
    encoded value.

Returns:
  Whether the value was mutated or not.

methods.name: flatbuffers.flexbuffers.Ref.AsFloat
documentation: Returns current reference as floating point value.

methods.name: flatbuffers.flexbuffers.Ref.MutateFloat
documentation:
Mutates underlying floating point value bytes in place.

Args:
  value: New float value. It must fit to the byte size of the existing
    encoded value.

Returns:
  Whether the value was mutated or not.

methods.name: flatbuffers.flexbuffers.Ref.Value
documentation:
Converts current reference to value of corresponding type.

This is equivalent to calling `AsInt` for integer values, `AsFloat` for
floating point values, etc.

Returns:
  Value of corresponding type.
Data accessor for the encoded data bytes.

classes.name: flatbuffers.flexbuffers.Value

methods.name: flatbuffers.flexbuffers.Value.ElemWidth
documentation: We have an absolute offset, but want to store a relative offset
elem_index elements beyond the current buffer end. Since whether
the relative offset fits in a certain byte_width depends on
the size of the elements before it (and their alignment), we have
to test for each size in turn.
Class to represent given value during the encoding process.

classes.name: flatbuffers.flexbuffers.Pool
documentation: Collection of (data, offset) pairs sorted by data for quick access.

classes.name: flatbuffers.flexbuffers.Builder

methods.name: flatbuffers.flexbuffers.Builder.Finish
documentation: Finishes encoding process and returns underlying buffer.

methods.name: flatbuffers.flexbuffers.Builder._WriteScalarVector
documentation: Writes scalar vector elements to the underlying buffer.

methods.name: flatbuffers.flexbuffers.Builder._CreateVector
documentation: Writes vector elements to the underlying buffer.

methods.name: flatbuffers.flexbuffers.Builder.String
documentation: Encodes string value.

methods.name: flatbuffers.flexbuffers.Builder.Blob
documentation:
Encodes binary blob value.

Args:
  value: A byte/bytearray value to encode

Returns:
  Offset of the encoded value in underlying the byte buffer.

methods.name: flatbuffers.flexbuffers.Builder.Key
documentation:
Encodes key value.

Args:
  value: A byte/bytearray/str value to encode. Byte object must not contain
    zero bytes. String object must be convertible to ASCII.

Returns:
  Offset of the encoded value in the underlying byte buffer.

methods.name: flatbuffers.flexbuffers.Builder.Null
documentation: Encodes None value.

methods.name: flatbuffers.flexbuffers.Builder.Bool
documentation:
Encodes boolean value.

Args:
  value: A boolean value.

methods.name: flatbuffers.flexbuffers.Builder.Int
documentation:
Encodes signed integer value.

Args:
  value: A signed integer value.
  byte_width: Number of bytes to use: 1, 2, 4, or 8.

methods.name: flatbuffers.flexbuffers.Builder.IndirectInt
documentation:
Encodes signed integer value indirectly.

Args:
  value: A signed integer value.
  byte_width: Number of bytes to use: 1, 2, 4, or 8.

methods.name: flatbuffers.flexbuffers.Builder.UInt
documentation:
Encodes unsigned integer value.

Args:
  value: An unsigned integer value.
  byte_width: Number of bytes to use: 1, 2, 4, or 8.

methods.name: flatbuffers.flexbuffers.Builder.IndirectUInt
documentation:
Encodes unsigned integer value indirectly.

Args:
  value: An unsigned integer value.
  byte_width: Number of bytes to use: 1, 2, 4, or 8.

methods.name: flatbuffers.flexbuffers.Builder.Float
documentation:
Encodes floating point value.

Args:
  value: A floating point value.
  byte_width: Number of bytes to use: 4 or 8.

methods.name: flatbuffers.flexbuffers.Builder.IndirectFloat
documentation:
Encodes floating point value indirectly.

Args:
  value: A floating point value.
  byte_width: Number of bytes to use: 4 or 8.

methods.name: flatbuffers.flexbuffers.Builder._StartVector
documentation: Starts vector construction.

methods.name: flatbuffers.flexbuffers.Builder._EndVector
documentation: Finishes vector construction by encodung its elements.

methods.name: flatbuffers.flexbuffers.Builder.VectorFromElements
documentation:
Encodes sequence of any elements as a vector.

Args:
  elements: sequence of elements, they may have different types.

methods.name: flatbuffers.flexbuffers.Builder.TypedVectorFromElements
documentation:
Encodes sequence of elements of the same type as typed vector.

Args:
  elements: Sequence of elements, they must be of the same type.
  element_type: Suggested element type. Setting it to None means determining
    correct value automatically based on the given elements.

methods.name: flatbuffers.flexbuffers.Builder.FixedTypedVectorFromElements
documentation:
Encodes sequence of elements of the same type as fixed typed vector.

Args:
  elements: Sequence of elements, they must be of the same type. Allowed
    types are `Type.INT`, `Type.UINT`, `Type.FLOAT`. Allowed number of
    elements are 2, 3, or 4.
  element_type: Suggested element type. Setting it to None means determining
    correct value automatically based on the given elements.
  byte_width: Number of bytes to use per element. For `Type.INT` and
    `Type.UINT`: 1, 2, 4, or 8. For `Type.FLOAT`: 4 or 8. Setting it to 0
    means determining correct value automatically based on the given
    elements.

methods.name: flatbuffers.flexbuffers.Builder._StartMap
documentation: Starts map construction.

methods.name: flatbuffers.flexbuffers.Builder._EndMap
documentation: Finishes map construction by encodung its elements.

methods.name: flatbuffers.flexbuffers.Builder.Add
documentation: Encodes value of any supported type.
Helper class to encode structural data into flexbuffers format.
